name: The Legend of Zelda
description: Nintendo's long-running action-adventure franchise — Link, Princess Zelda, Ganon, and the Triforce.

questions.q: Which Japanese video-game company develops and publishes The Legend of Zelda?

source.notes: First released for the Famicom Disk System in 1986.

questions.q: Who is the hero Link's primary weapon in almost every Zelda game?

source.notes: The legendary Master Sword in most titles.

questions.q: What is the name of the kingdom where most Zelda games take place?

source.notes: Sometimes spelled Hylia's name forms.

questions.q: Who is the series' primary antagonist, a demon king that threatens Hyrule?

source.notes: Also appears as his humanoid form Ganondorf.

questions.q: What is the Triforce — the sacred relic at the heart of most Zelda plots — made of?

source.notes: Ganon usually holds Power, Zelda Wisdom, Link Courage.

questions.q: What race is Link himself?

source.notes: Hylians have the distinctive pointed ears.

questions.q: What currency do players collect in Zelda games?

source.notes: Gem-shaped; different colors have different values.

questions.q: Who created the Zelda franchise?

source.notes: Alongside Takashi Tezuka.

questions.q: What is the name of Link's horse, introduced in Ocarina of Time?

source.notes: Named after the Celtic goddess of horses.

questions.q: What year was the original 'The Legend of Zelda' first released in Japan?

source.notes: Released in North America the following year.

questions.q: Which Nintendo 64 Zelda game, released in 1998, is often ranked among the best video games of all time?

source.notes: Introduced the Z-targeting system.

questions.q: Which Zelda game introduced a time-based three-day loop on a doomed moon?

source.original_a: A time-based concept where Link relives three days repeatedly
source.notes: Majora's Mask (2000) — a direct sequel to Ocarina of Time.

questions.q: The 2017 open-world Zelda title that launched with the Switch is called what?

source.notes: Released March 3, 2017.

questions.q: What is the name of the 2023 direct sequel to Breath of the Wild?

source.notes: Released May 12, 2023.

questions.q: Which longtime composer has scored most Zelda games, from the original onward?

source.notes: Also wrote the Super Mario Bros. theme.

questions.q: In many Zelda games, what small fairy-like character accompanies Link with advice?

source.notes: Navi from Ocarina of Time — 'Hey! Listen!'

questions.q: Which mountain-dwelling stone race is a recurring ally in Zelda games?

source.notes: Gorons eat rocks and love sumo.

questions.q: Which Zelda game takes place mostly at sea, with Link sailing between islands?

source.notes: GameCube, 2002; cel-shaded graphics.

questions.q: Who voices adult Link's occasional grunts in most modern games?

source.notes: Kengo Takanashi voiced Link in Breath of the Wild and Tears of the Kingdom.

questions.q: The 2019 remake of Link's Awakening for Switch is notable for its what kind of look?

source.notes: Characters look like small plastic toys on a diorama.

questions.q: The first Zelda title to feature the name 'Zelda' as a playable protagonist was 2024's what?
choices: The Legend of Zelda: Age of Calamity
The Legend of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom
The Legend of Zelda: Echoes of Wisdom

source.original_a: The Legend of Zelda: Echoes of Wisdom
source.notes: Released September 26, 2024 — the first mainline game where Zelda is the lead protagonist.

questions.q: The series' canonical timeline, confirmed in 'Hyrule Historia,' famously branches due to which game's ending?

source.notes: Ocarina of Time splits into three timelines: Adult, Child, Failed.
